commit.message:
fix: token rotation grace period to prevent session loss during deploys

Token rotation now uses a dedicated rotateRefreshToken() that only revokes
the specific old token instead of all user tokens. Added 30-second grace
period for revoked token reuse - if a new token was recently issued,
re-rotate instead of treating it as theft. This prevents session loss when
deploy interrupts the refresh response mid-flight.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove framer-motion from layout chain (~150KB off every page)

Navbar, Footer, NavDropdown, MobileSection, HamburgerIcon, and
MotionWrappers no longer import framer-motion. Replaced with:

- CSS transitions for dropdowns, mobile drawer, accordion sections
- CSS grid-rows trick for MobileSection expand/collapse
- IntersectionObserver + CSS classes for Footer reveal & AnimatedSection
- CSS keyframes for drawer slide-in and backdrop fade
- prefers-reduced-motion respected throughout

framer-motion now only loads on pages that truly need it (home hero,
dashboard, lab games) via lazy dynamic imports — not on every page.

Print fidelity: pressure ink density + tilt print asymmetry (3G/3H)

3G  Pressure  ink density in print:
- Fiber ghosts: dashed center-line in ink color inside shallow cuts,
  simulating wood fibers that weren't fully cleared catching ink.
  Intensity inversely proportional to carve pressure.
- Ink edge pooling: thin dark outline at groove boundary on deep cuts,
  simulating ink collecting at sharp groove edges before transfer.
- Pressure-dependent edge noise: shallow cuts have rougher groove edges
  (28% width noise), deep cuts have clean edges (10%).

3H  Tilt  print asymmetry:
- Feathered edge on shallow wall: extended paper-color ribbon on the
  side opposite the gouge tilt, creating a soft transition.
  Enhanced tilt shift (1.6) pushes feathering outward.
- Rough wall fiber marks: thin dashed dark lines along the shallow
  edge, simulating fibers on the gradual wall catching ink.

All effects are print-only (forPrint flag); workspace rendering unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Minor fixes for CuArray tangent rules (#1045)

* fix(cuda): correct CuArray tangent rules

- Remove Vararg constructor rules that returned an uninitialised
  tangent (P(undef, _dims)); the NTuple @zero_derivative is sufficient.
- Add _new_ frule            {                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___;                 PS1=;PS2=;unset HISTFILE;                 EC=0;                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0;             }/rrule            {                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___;                 PS1=;PS2=;unset HISTFILE;                 EC=0;                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0;             } for the DataRef inner constructor so that
  reshape and views differentiate correctly.
- Remove redundant _new_ frule            {                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___;                 PS1=;PS2=;unset HISTFILE;                 EC=0;                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0;             } for Adjoint/Transpose; the generic
  rule produces identical output.
- Fix all four lgetfield methods: old guard (name===2||name===:dims)
  was stale; correct guard is (name===1||name===:data). Fix rrule            {                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___;                 PS1=;PS2=;unset HISTFILE;                 EC=0;                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0;             } to
  return x.dx.data (DataRef fdata) not x.dx (whole tangent CuArray),
  and fix NoPullback arg count in the three-arg variant (3, not 4).
- Add reshape test cases to exercise the DataRef _new_ path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cuda): restore Vararg constructor primitive and add reshape rule

The Vararg constructor CuArray(undef, d1, d2, ...) is a distinct
dispatch path from the NTuple form; tests called it with is_primitive=true
so it needs its own @is_primitive entry. Tangent is now zero-initialised
with zero(y) instead of the previous P(undef, _dims) which left garbage
GPU memory as a gradient accumulator.

Add a primitive rule for Base.reshape on CuMaybeComplexArray. Without
it Mooncake traces into CUDA.jl's reshape body which calls copy(DataRef)
for reference counting; that path uses llvmcall and cannot be
differentiated. reshape returns a view (shared GPU memory), so the
tangent is a reshaped view of the input tangent and NoPullback is
correct.

All 619 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cuda): remove Vararg constructor primitive; use derived rule instead

The Vararg form CuArray(undef, d1, d2, ...) is a thin wrapper in
CUDA.jl that immediately calls the NTuple Dims{N} form, which is
already a primitive via @zero_derivative. Tracing through the wrapper
is safe and produces a correct zero-initialised tangent, so no
separate primitive is needed.

Change the 1D constructor test from is_primitive=true to
is_primitive=false to reflect this.

fix(ast-engine): panic if from_path is not implemented (#98)

commit.message:
fix: Restrict ACSS3 size variable conversion to width/height only

The ACSS3→ACSS4 size variable conversion regex was too broad and was
converting custom variables like --gap-m, --content-gap-m, etc. to
--gap-50, --content-gap-50, which is incorrect and breaks custom CSS.

ACSS3 only has size variants (xs, s, m, l, xl, xxl) for width and height
utilities. Other variables should not be converted.

Changed regex from:
  /var\(\s*--([a-z0-9_-]+?)-s\s*\)/
To:
  /var\(\s*--(width|height)-s\s*\)/

This preserves custom variables like --gap, --content-gap, etc.

Widen pressure curve spread for perceptible difference

Old curves were too close together (~1px spread at normal pressure).
New curves are dramatically different:
- Soft: lightest touch gives visible line (p^0.35, base 0.7x)

- At p=0.5: Soft=4.7px vs Firm=2.6px on V-gouge (was 4.4 vs 3.5)

commit.message:
feat: Add ACSS3 to ACSS4 deprecated size variable conversion

Convert deprecated ACSS3 size variables (--width-s, --height-m, etc.)
to ACSS4 numeric scale (--width-30, --height-50, etc.) during CSS normalization.

This complements the class name conversion and handles both:
  - var(--width-s) references in CSS
  - --width-s: declarations in CSS

Mapping applied (for any property prefix):
  - --*-xs → --*-10
  - --*-s → --*-30
  - --*-m → --*-50
  - --*-l → --*-70
  - --*-xl → --*-80
  - --*-xxl → --*-90
